claim: 95% accuracy on agent outputs is table stakes and insufficient for adoption; agents require personalization and delegation beyond accuracy to drive user engagement
actionable: Focus on personalization, delegation features, and workflow integration rather than chasing accuracy metrics above 95% threshold

claim: Bolting AI onto broken workflows accelerates failure; must redesign workflows as AI-native before deploying agents to avoid compounding existing process issues
actionable: Audit and redesign workflow before deploying agents; don't layer agents on top of fundamentally broken processes

claim: Supervisor graph architecture with planners enables dynamic task decomposition based on context; planner generates multi-stage execution plans with reflection/replan loops
actionable: Implement planner agents that dynamically decompose user requests into multi-stage task graphs with built-in reflection and re-planning

claim: Keep supervisors lean; focus on metadata/policy routing only, not complex reasoning; use domain-specific planners inside subgraphs for task decomposition
actionable: Separate routing logic (supervisor) from task planning (planner) to maintain clear architecture and avoid bloated supervisors

claim: LLMs become creative and deviate from instructions over multiple steps; use deterministic task workflows with hardcoded decision trees for critical business logic
actionable: Separate deterministic business rules (use code/workflows) from reasoning tasks (use LLM); don't rely on LLM instruction-following for multi-step deterministic tasks

claim: Acronym disambiguation and context specification are critical early planning steps; agents need explicit understanding of domain terminology (e.g., ATR = Available To Renew)
actionable: Include explicit disambiguation and context specification steps in agent planning to resolve domain-specific terminology before task execution

claim: Self-correction and reflection loops are non-optional in production; agents must validate outputs and replan if execution deviates from task requirements
actionable: Build reflection/replan loops into agent workflows; agents should validate outputs and iterate before returning results

claim: Humans will revert to previous tools unless agents are embedded in daily workflows and provide autonomous delegation; optionality kills adoption
actionable: Integrate agents directly into existing workflows; don't present as optional tools users can choose to bypass

claim: Shift from human-orchestrated workflows to AI-driven execution with confidence-based human handoff improves autonomy; humans judge outcomes, not intermediate steps
actionable: Design agentic workflows where AI executes autonomously and humans intervene only on low-confidence decisions, not at every step

claim: Routing decision (which agent/task to use) is harder and more important than agent reasoning; get routing right before optimizing agent capability
actionable: Prioritize routing correctness in agent systems; invest in clear routing logic before expanding agent capabilities
